verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 is a soluble mitochondrial matrix chaperone (Hsp60) that is secondarily detected at the cell surface and in extracellular space via secretion/release, not via a membrane anchor, GPI linkage, or stable TM-partner complex. While ~15-20% of the protein can localize to discrete extramitochondrial sites in some cells, the surface pool appears to be a consequence of release rather than a constitutive surface-facing mechanism intrinsic to the protein. No stable physical linkage to the membrane has been documented. The protein would not remain on the outer leaflet after washing.
